evals.prompt:
I have a Python bug in my order processing module. It crashes in production with a KeyError about 10% of the time. Traceback:

  File "orders.py", line 5, in calculate_total
    discount = order['metadata']['promo_code']['discount_pct']
KeyError: 'promo_code'

The file is at C:\agent-dev\skills\diagnose-workspace\iteration-1\eval-1-python-keyerror\input\orders.py

Please diagnose and fix this bug.
evals.expected_output: Agent follows 6-phase debugging methodology: builds a feedback loop, reproduces the crash, generates 3+ falsifiable hypotheses, instruments with tagged logs, writes regression test before fix, and produces a Diagnosis Summary + Post-Mortem.
expectations: Agent creates a test script that reproduces the KeyError before attempting any fix
Agent generates at least 3 explicitly stated hypotheses before testing them
Agent uses [DEBUG-XXXX] prefixed log statements for instrumentation
Agent writes a regression test before or as part of applying the fix
Agent correctly identifies root cause: metadata dict exists but lacks promo_code key
Output contains a 'Diagnosis Summary' section with Root Cause field
Output contains a 'Post-Mortem' section with Prevention field
Agent reads references/feedback-loops.md (with_skill only)

evals.prompt:
My Python counter module has a concurrency bug. When 20 threads each call increment() once, the final value is consistently less than 20 — lost updates due to a race condition.

The file is at C:\agent-dev\skills\diagnose-workspace\iteration-1\eval-2-race-condition\input\counter.py

Please diagnose and fix this bug.
evals.expected_output: Agent follows 6-phase methodology: builds a concurrent stress harness, reproduces the lost-update pattern, generates 3+ hypotheses, instruments with tagged logs, writes a concurrency regression test first, and produces Diagnosis Summary + Post-Mortem.
expectations: Agent creates a concurrent stress test harness that reliably reproduces the lost-update bug
Agent identifies the root cause as non-atomic read-modify-write (TOCTOU race)
Agent generates at least 3 explicitly stated hypotheses
Agent uses [DEBUG-XXXX] prefixed log statements during instrumentation phase
Agent writes a concurrency regression test before applying the fix
Output contains a 'Diagnosis Summary' section with Root Cause field
Output contains a 'Post-Mortem' section with Prevention field

evals.prompt:
My Python search module has a performance regression. It used to return in ~20ms but now takes 2-4 seconds after the table grew from ~5k to ~200k rows. No code was changed.

The file is at C:\agent-dev\skills\diagnose-workspace\iteration-1\eval-3-performance-regression\input\search.py

Please diagnose and fix the performance issue.
evals.expected_output: Agent follows 6-phase methodology: builds a timing harness (NOT regular logging), runs query EXPLAIN to confirm full table scan, generates 3+ hypotheses ranked by Bayesian prior, instruments with profiling tools, creates performance regression test, and outputs Diagnosis Summary + Post-Mortem.
expectations: Agent creates a timing measurement harness (perf_counter or equivalent), not regular print logging
